title: Container fails to start after attempting to chmod a symlink when run via Kubernetes YAML
body:
### Issue Description

I have a container that I can run perfectly fine on the command-line with `podman run`, but with fails to start when using `podman play kube` due to the following error.

```
[starting container 5aaa9c2c89ac91dc4bb02c8e7e3adac8e7d29be0f19eac65487eda77ee460029: crun: chmod `run/icinga2`: Operation not supported: OCI runtime error]
Pod:
2375c1c815fc7eb20eb71d9786059eaef51974d1d7c8259992536d550ab97a0e
Container:
5aaa9c2c89ac91dc4bb02c8e7e3adac8e7d29be0f19eac65487eda77ee460029

starting container 5aaa9c2c89ac91dc4bb02c8e7e3adac8e7d29be0f19eac65487eda77ee460029: crun: chmod `run/icinga2`: Operation not supported: OCI runtime error

Error: failed to start 1 containers
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run the container.
```
podman run \
                     --env ICINGA_MASTER=1 \
                     --hostname icinga.jwillikers.io \
                     --detach \
                     --name icinga \
                     --rm \
                     --tty \
                     --volume icinga-data:/data:Z \
                     --volume ~/Projects/icinga-config/etc/icinga2:/data/etc/icinga2:ro,Z \
                     docker.io/icinga/icinga2:latest \
                     icinga2 daemon -x debug
```

2. Generate Kubernetes YAML with `podman generate kube -f icinga.yaml -s icinga`.
I've added the SELinux option `type: spc_t` in the following YAML.

```
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.7.0

# NOTE: If you generated this yaml from an unprivileged and rootless podman container on an SELinux
# enabled system, check the podman generate kube man page for steps to follow to ensure that your pod/container
# has the right permissions to access the volumes added.
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2023-11-12T16:36:15Z"
  labels:
    app: icinga-pod
  name: icinga-pod
spec:
  selector:
    app: icinga-pod
  type: NodePort
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    bind-mount-options: /var/home/core/Projects/icinga-config/etc/icinga2:Z
  creationTimestamp: "2023-11-12T16:36:15Z"
  labels:
    app: icinga-pod
  name: icinga-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - args:
    - icinga2
    - daemon
    - -x
    - debug
    env:
    - name: TERM
      value: xterm
    - name: ICINGA_MASTER
      value: "1"
    - name: HOSTNAME
      value: icinga.jwillikers.io
    image: docker.io/icinga/icinga2:latest
    name: icinga
    securityContext:
      seLinuxOptions:
        type: spc_t
    tty: true
    volumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /data/etc/icinga2
      name: var-home-core-Projects-icinga-config-etc-icinga2-host-0
      readOnly: true
    - mountPath: /data
      name: icinga-data-pvc
  hostname: icinga.jwillikers.io
  volumes:
  - hostPath:
      path: /var/home/core/Projects/icinga-config/etc/icinga2
      type: Directory
    name: var-home-core-Projects-icinga-config-etc-icinga2-host-0
  - name: icinga-data-pvc
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: icinga-data
```

4. Attempt to run the YAML with `podman kube play ./icinga.yaml`


### Describe the results you received

```
[starting container 109fb530dd964407106906038e419441eb7183ea3f2eaea987e4734b08704c69: crun: chmod `run/icinga2`: Operation not supported: OCI runtime error]
Pod:
c1ce30b80058631349a635cde47a5e9f5884ba269058b8ff8d9df8f0c14d9047
Container:
109fb530dd964407106906038e419441eb7183ea3f2eaea987e4734b08704c69

starting container 109fb530dd964407106906038e419441eb7183ea3f2eaea987e4734b08704c69: crun: chmod `run/icinga2`: Operation not supported: OCI runtime error
```

### Describe the results you expected

It should run with a warning and not failure like when using `podman run`.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Fedora IoT 39
aarch64

### Additional information

Likely relates to containers/crun#1308 / containers/crun#1309.

comments.body: As mentioned in the crun issue you need to update crun (at least 1.9.1) to have this fixed. There is nothing we can do on the podman side.
